job_title: Manager, Data Science - AI for Data
strengths: Strong Python and SQL hands-on experience
Proven ETL/ELT and data pipeline implementation
Solid visualization and reporting (Tableau, Power BI)
weaknesses: Limited explicit ML/deep learning production experience
No clear experience with Spark or H2O
NLP/LLM and transformer work not demonstrated
Less than preferred years of senior data science experience
improvement_bullets: Leveraged a broad stack of technologies including Python, SQL, AWS (S3), Snowflake, and BigQuery to process and analyze large volumes of data, building robust ETL/ELT pipelines for reporting and analysis.

Developed and validated machine learning models for customer segmentation and campaign analysis, including clustering and classification techniques, utilizing Python and open-source libraries to inform marketing strategies.
Designed and executed A/B tests for digital marketing campaigns, providing statistical evidence for audience response and lift, demonstrating proficiency in evaluation and validation methodologies.

Integrated data from APIs and PostgreSQL databases to build structured datasets, supporting downstream analytics and reporting, with a focus on data quality and accuracy through standardized KPIs and governance frameworks.

Built Python automation for weekly sales and operations reports, ingesting POS and CRM data, saving 8 hours per week and improving reporting accuracy, demonstrating strong data pipeline and automation skills.
cover_letter:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I am excited to submit my application for the Manager, Data Science - AI for Data opportunity with Capital One. I am interested in this opportunity because Capital One's pioneering history in data-driven disruption and its current leadership in leveraging advanced AI/ML technologies across billions of customer records deeply resonates with my passion for transforming complex data into impactful financial solutions.

What interests me about working for Capital One is its approach to using cutting-edge AI/ML technologies to enhance financial lives. This excites, inspires, and intrigues me because I am driven by the potential to innovate and create next-generation data products that directly benefit everyday people. I believe my proficiency in Python and SQL, coupled with my experience in statistical modeling and data pipeline development, would make me a strong candidate for this role.

During my professional career, I have approached problems with a data-first mindset, consistently translating complex technical findings into actionable business recommendations. In fact, at Shine Masters Cleaning Services, I developed statistical models to identify high-value customer segments. This initiative, informed by targeted marketing, contributed to a 300% increase in revenue in the targeted segment over 12 months. The result showcased the power of data-driven segmentation to drive significant business growth.

Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead at Shine Masters Cleaning Services enabled me to develop my automation and reporting skills. I built Python automation for weekly sales and operations reports, ingesting POS and CRM data, saving 8 hours per week and improving reporting accuracy for faster strategic decisions. This experience highlights my ability to enhance efficiency and reliability in data operations.

I’m excited by the opportunity to use my statistical modeling and machine learning capabilities in working on developing AI-enabled features across the data lifecycle at Capital One. I know that my ability to partner with cross-functional teams and my strong technical foundation align with what it takes to succeed in the Manager, Data Science - AI for Data role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail.

Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Warm regards,
Justin Miller
